text:
The paper has a dedicated OpenReview page with the exact matching title. 
The paper "MEGA: A Large-Scale Molecular Editing Dataset for Guided-Action Optimization" exists on OpenReview, introducing a large-scale dataset of molecule pairs for property-improving chemical edits.
 
It was submitted to the AI for Science workshop at NeurIPS 2025.
 However, the OpenReview submission is listed as from "Anonymous Author(s)," so the specific cited authors (Nelson Fernandez, Air Liquide, Maxime Illouz, Luis Pinto, Entao Yang, Habiboulaye Amadou Boubacar) cannot be confirmed from the available web sources at this time. "Air Liquide" appears to be an affiliation listed as an author, which is a metadata irregularity. Despite the author-list uncertainty, the paper itself clearly exists at the cited venue.

EXPLANATION: The paper exists with this exact title on OpenReview as a submission to the AI4Science NeurIPS 2025 workshop; however, the submission is anonymized so the cited authors cannot be confirmed. "Air Liquide" appears to be a misformatted affiliation rather than an author name, suggesting metadata formatting issues rather than fabrication.
LINK: https://openreview.net/forum?id=wzou4rm3Tt
FOUND_TITLE: MEGA: A Large-Scale Molecular Editing Dataset for Guided-Action Optimization
FOUND_AUTHORS: Anonymous (under review)
FOUND_YEAR: 2025
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
